---
title: ""
pagetitle: "AWI Earth System Complexity"
page-layout: full
---

::: {.hero}
![](assets/logo.png){.hero-logo fig-alt="AWI Earth System Complexity logo"}

The Earth System Complexity group at the Alfred Wegener Institute investigates
the dynamics, feedbacks, and tipping behaviour of the climate system across
timescales — from past glacial cycles to future projections.
:::

## What we do

We combine process-based modelling, Earth observation, and statistical methods
to understand how complexity emerges in the climate system. Our work spans
ice-sheet dynamics, paleoclimate reconstruction, ocean–cryosphere interactions,
and the development of efficient simulation tools.

## Where to find us

Alfred Wegener Institute, Helmholtz Centre for Polar and Marine Research
Bremerhaven, Germany
